twitter_thread: 40% of 'top 10' dividend stocks have payout ratios above 90%, including ExxonMobil and AT&T.
Gordon Growth Model reveals 3M's dividend growth is likely 5%, not 10%, due to slowing sales.
Filter out dividend stocks with debt-to-equity ratios above 0.7, like Ford Motor Company.
Only 12 S&P 500 dividend stocks have increased payouts for 10+ consecutive years, including Procter & Gamble.
AT&T's 4.4% yield is a dividend trap, masking a 120% payout ratio and declining revenue.
What tool do you use to screen for hidden dividend risks, like Enbridge's 130% payout ratio?
What metric do you prioritize when evaluating dividend stocks, like Coca-Cola's 3.5% yield? #passiveincome #dividends
